description_long: This is a traditional stone lantern, reminiscent of those found in Japanese gardens and temple grounds. The structure features a tall, square pedestal base supporting a central housing with open windows on each side, allowing light to emanate outward. Above the housing sits a broad, gently sloped roof with a prominent finial or ornamental sphere at the apex. The lantern is constructed from a uniform, gray stone material, giving it a solid and weathered appearance. The design is geometric and symmetrical, with clean lines and subtle stepped details beneath the roof. There are no visible decorative engravings or colored elements, emphasizing a minimalist and functional aesthetic.
description: A tall, gray stone lantern with a square pedestal, open windowed housing, and a broad, sloped roof topped by a spherical finial. The design is geometric, minimalist, and traditional.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square pedestal, open windowed housing, and the full width of the sloped roof with the central finial clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same pedestal, housing, and roof proportions visible, highlighting the lantern's symmetrical design.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same open windowed housing and roof structure, showing no additional features or decorations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again nearly identical to the other side, emphasizing the lantern's uniformity and symmetry.
